description: Phase 2 Worktree Lifecycle Management: cleanup on loop/batch exit, early exit cleanup, worktrees command, enhanced lock metadata, session banner hints, multi-PRD status, per-PRD progress files.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test: remove_worktree() on clean worktree returns Ok(true) and path no longer exists
Test: remove_worktree() on dirty worktree returns Ok(false) and prints warning
Test: remove_worktree() removes empty parent dir after last worktree removed
Test: ensure_worktree() cleans up empty parent dir on git worktree add failure
Test: ensure_worktree() calls git worktree prune on partial failure
Known-bad discriminator: remove_worktree() with non-existent path returns error (not Ok(true))
Tests compile (may use #[ignore] where implementation doesn't exist yet)
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for remove_worktree() in env.rs and early exit cleanup in ensure_worktree(). Cover happy path removal, dirty worktree skip, partial creation cleanup, and git worktree prune on failure.
edgeCases: Worktree with uncommitted changes: git worktree remove fails, must warn and return false
Worktree directory deleted out-of-band: git worktree prune should clean metadata
Empty {repo}-worktrees/ parent dir: should be removed after last worktree removed
Non-existent worktree path: should return error, not silently succeed

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return Ok(false), print warning to stderr
Run git worktree prune, remove empty parent if created

invariants: remove_worktree() never deletes a worktree with uncommitted changes
Successful removal always followed by git worktree prune
ensure_worktree() error path never leaves orphaned empty directories
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Use existing setup_git_repo() test helper from env.rs. Implementation tasks FEAT-001 and FEAT-002 depend on these.
userStories.title: Initial tests for remove_worktree() and early exit cleanup

acceptanceCriteria: Test: write_holder_info_extended() writes multi-line format with branch/worktree/prefix
Test: read_holder_info() parses new multi-line format into HolderInfo struct
Test: read_holder_info() falls back to old single-line pid@host format (backwards compat)
Test: read_holder_info() returns None for empty file
Test: lock error message includes branch + prefix when available
Known-bad discriminator: old format returns HolderInfo with None for branch/worktree/prefix (not empty strings)
Tests compile (may use #[ignore] where implementation doesn't exist yet)
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for HolderInfo struct, write_holder_info_extended(), and updated read_holder_info() in lock.rs. Cover new multi-line format writing/reading and backwards compatibility with old single-line format.
edgeCases: Lock file in old single-line format: must parse first line as identity, optionals all None
Lock file with partial metadata (e.g., branch but no prefix): parse what exists, None for rest
Lock file with extra unknown key=value lines: ignore them gracefully

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Parse as identity-only, all metadata fields None

invariants: read_holder_info() always succeeds on old-format lock files (backwards compat)
HolderInfo.identity always contains pid@host regardless of format
write_holder_info_extended() first line is always pid@host (for old readers)
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Existing lock.rs tests provide patterns. FEAT-003 depends on these.
userStories.title: Initial tests for enhanced lock file metadata

acceptanceCriteria: Test: print_session_banner() with all hints (DB, stop, pause, worktree) doesn't panic
Test: print_session_banner() without worktree path omits worktree line
Test: print_session_banner() with prefix uses .stop-{prefix} in hint
Test: resolve_paths() with prefix returns progress-{prefix}.txt
Test: resolve_paths() without prefix returns progress.txt (legacy)
Known-bad discriminator: resolve_paths with prefix='P1' returns 'progress-P1.txt' not 'progress.txt'
Tests compile
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for enhanced print_session_banner() with path hints, and for resolve_paths() with optional prefix parameter for per-PRD progress files.
edgeCases: No prefix in legacy DB: progress file falls back to progress.txt
Prefix with special characters: sanitized in filename
Banner with very long paths: truncated to fit fixed-width box

failureModes.cause: Very long worktree path in banner
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Truncated with ... to fit box width

invariants: resolve_paths() without prefix always returns progress.txt
Banner output always goes to stderr, never stdout
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Existing display.rs and env.rs test patterns apply. FEAT-004 and FEAT-005 depend on these.
userStories.title: Initial tests for session banner hints and per-PRD progress files

acceptanceCriteria: If git worktree add fails and parent dir was just created (empty), parent is removed
If git worktree add partially fails, git worktree prune is called
Successful worktree creation path is unchanged
All TEST-INIT-001 early-exit tests pass
userStories.description: Add cleanup on error paths in ensure_worktree(). If git worktree add fails after parent dir was created, remove the empty parent and run git worktree prune. No change to the successful creation path.
userStories.notes: Group A (standalone). Only modify the error path after line 449 in env.rs where create_result.status.success() is false. Track whether we created the parent dir to know if cleanup is needed.

correctness: Must not remove parent dir if it existed before ensure_worktree() was called
Must not remove parent dir if it contains other worktrees
performance: Best effort — git commands are fast
style: Follow existing TaskMgrResult error propagation pattern

userStories.title: US-P2-002: Early exit worktree cleanup in ensure_worktree()

acceptanceCriteria: remove_worktree() calls git worktree remove from source_root
On success, calls git worktree prune
On failure (uncommitted changes), prints warning to stderr and returns Ok(false)
Removes empty {repo}-worktrees/ parent dir if it becomes empty
parse_worktree_list() and compute_worktree_path() are pub(crate)
All TEST-INIT-001 removal tests pass
userStories.description: Add pub fn remove_worktree(source_root, worktree_path) -> TaskMgrResult<bool> to env.rs. Runs git worktree remove, then git worktree prune. Returns true if removed, false if skipped (dirty). Also make parse_worktree_list() and compute_worktree_path() pub(crate) for reuse.
userStories.notes: Group A (standalone). This function is the foundation for US-P2-001, US-P2-003, US-P2-005. Pattern: follow ensure_worktree() style for git Command usage.

correctness: Must never delete a worktree with uncommitted changes
Must handle non-existent worktree path gracefully
style: Use Command::new('git') pattern consistent with existing env.rs functions

userStories.title: US-P2-002: Add remove_worktree() utility function

branch=X
worktree=Y

read_holder_info() returns Option<HolderInfo> (breaking change from Option<String>)

userStories.description: Add HolderInfo struct, write_holder_info_extended() method, and update read_holder_info() in lock.rs. Lock files become multi-line with branch/worktree/prefix metadata. Backwards-compatible with old single-line format.
userStories.notes: Group A (standalone). Breaking change to read_holder_info() return type — update the single caller in acquire_inner() at line 76. First line of new format is always pid@host for old-reader compat.

correctness: Old lock files must still be readable — backwards compatibility is mandatory
First line of new format must always be pid@host
style: HolderInfo fields use Option<String> for metadata (not empty strings)
Parse unknown key=value lines gracefully (skip, don't error)

userStories.notes: Group A (standalone). Breaking change to print_session_banner() signature — only one caller at engine.rs:831. Box width may need to increase for longer hint lines.

correctness: Banner must not panic on very long paths — truncate
style: Use struct param instead of many positional args

userStories.description: Add optional prefix parameter to resolve_paths() in env.rs. When prefix is present, progress file becomes progress-{prefix}.txt. Falls back to progress.txt for legacy/no-prefix mode.
userStories.notes: Group A (standalone). Breaking change to resolve_paths() — update caller in engine.rs. The progress::rotate_progress() call at engine.rs:857 already uses paths.progress_file so it will automatically use the prefixed path.

correctness: Must not break existing progress.txt when no prefix is used
Existing progress.txt continues to work (no migration needed)

userStories.notes: Group B. Depends on FEAT-003 (enhanced lock) being complete. The lock is acquired early in run_loop() — update that call site.

correctness: Lock must still be released properly on Drop
style: Pass metadata as Option<&str> — None when not available yet

acceptanceCriteria: run_loop() returns LoopResult { exit_code: i32, worktree_path: Option<PathBuf> }

--cleanup-worktree flag added to Loop command in cli/commands.rs
cleanup_worktree field added to LoopConfig
On completion with --cleanup-worktree: calls remove_worktree()
On completion interactive: prompts user to remove worktree
On completion with --yes (no --cleanup-worktree): keeps worktree
If removal fails (dirty): warns but exits with original exit code

consumers.usage: Calls run_loop(), uses return as exit code

consumers.mitigation: Update to use .exit_code, also access .worktree_path for batch cleanup
consumers.usage: Calls run_loop(), stores exit_code in PrdRunResult

userStories.description: Add --cleanup-worktree flag to the loop command. On loop completion, optionally clean up the worktree. In interactive mode, prompt to remove. With --cleanup-worktree, force removal. In --yes mode without --cleanup-worktree, auto-keep. Return LoopResult struct from run_loop() instead of bare i32.
userStories.notes: Group B. Depends on FEAT-002 (remove_worktree). Insert cleanup logic after Step 21 in engine.rs (after deadline/signal cleanup, before final banner). The LoopResult return type change affects main.rs and batch.rs.

correctness: Must not error on cleanup failure — warn only
LoopResult must be returned even if cleanup fails
style: LoopResult struct in engine.rs, derives Debug
Cleanup logic in a separate helper function, not inline in run_loop()

acceptanceCriteria: Worktrees subcommand added to Commands enum in cli/commands.rs with List, Prune, Remove(target) actions
commands/worktrees.rs module with list(), prune(), remove() functions
list: parses git worktree list, cross-references lock files in .task-mgr/, shows branch+path+lock status
prune: removes worktrees with no active lock via remove_worktree(), then git worktree prune
remove: finds worktree by path or branch, calls remove_worktree()
WorktreesResult struct with format_text() for human-readable output
Command dispatched from main.rs
Module registered in commands/mod.rs and handlers.rs
userStories.description: Add new task-mgr worktrees subcommand with list, prune, and remove actions. List shows all worktrees with branch, path, and lock status. Prune removes unlocked worktrees. Remove targets a specific worktree by path or branch.
userStories.notes: Group C. Depends on MILESTONE-1 (remove_worktree + enhanced locks). Follow existing command module pattern: Result struct + format_text() + registration in mod.rs + handlers.rs TextFormattable impl.

correctness: prune must cross-reference lock files before removing — never remove an actively locked worktree
list must handle case where git worktree list returns bare repo as first entry
style: Register in handlers.rs via impl_text_formattable! macro

userStories.title: US-P2-003: Worktrees list/prune/remove command

acceptanceCriteria: --keep-worktrees flag added to Batch command in cli/commands.rs
batch.rs accesses LoopResult.worktree_path after each PRD run
On success + --yes: auto-remove worktree via remove_worktree()
On failure: keep worktree regardless of flags
With --keep-worktrees: never remove
Interactive (no --yes): prompt user to remove each worktree
Cleanup failure warns but doesn't affect batch result
userStories.description: After each PRD's loop completes in batch mode, offer to remove its worktree. In --yes mode, auto-remove on success, keep on failure. Add --keep-worktrees flag to batch command.
userStories.notes: Group C. Depends on FEAT-007 (LoopResult) being complete. Add cleanup logic after each engine::run_loop() call in the batch for-loop.

correctness: Must never remove worktree on failure — user needs to inspect
Cleanup failure must not change batch exit code
style: Follow existing batch.rs patterns for flag threading
